brief.headline: Fed day under a new chair, with oil finally cooling
brief.summary: All eyes are on the Federal Reserve's decision — the first chaired by Kevin Warsh — where rates are expected to hold and the real signal is how he reshapes the Fed's tone. The backdrop has shifted in markets' favour: the Strait of Hormuz has reopened and oil has dropped below $80, easing the inflation fears that drove this year's rate hikes. The dollar is steady ahead of the announcement, the euro has slipped under 1.1600, and the yen keeps falling even after the Bank of Japan's hike to 1.0%. UK inflation held steady rather than cooling, teeing up a tense Bank of England decision, while oil-importing currencies like the rupee and ringgit are catching a bid from cheaper crude.

pairsToWatch.note: Slipped below 1.1600 — the Fed press conference decides the next break.
Yen still weak despite the BoJ's hike to 1.0%; rate gaps to the dollar dominate.
Steady UK inflation makes the Bank of England's call a live one.
Loonie pinned near lows as oil slides below $80.

calendar: US Federal Reserve rate decision today — Kevin Warsh's first as chair; hold expected, focus on the press conference
Bank of England decision imminent after inflation held steady
Watch oil and Strait of Hormuz headlines — the swing factor for inflation and commodity currencies
brief.spendIdea: Amazon Prime Day runs June 23–27, but early deals are already live — Wirecutter's discounted Father's Day picks and AirPods Pro near their lowest mean there's no need to wait for the day itself.
brief.transferTip: With the Fed today and the Bank of England close behind, rates can swing around both announcements — if your transfer isn't urgent, the calmer windows between central bank events usually price better.

categories.label: World News
categories.description: Geopolitics, elections, trade and conflict — the world events that move currencies.

categories.label: Central Banks
categories.description: Rate decisions, interventions, QE and speeches from central banks anywhere in the world.

categories.label: FX Analysis
categories.description: Daily currency commentary and forecasts from banks and research desks.

categories.label: Market Movers
categories.description: Economic data, commodities and risk sentiment with a direct line to FX.

categories.label: Life & Money
categories.description: Spend it well — gifts for loved ones, new tech worth buying, and travel where your money goes further.

items.headline: Trump warns US will 'go back to shooting' if Iran misbehaves as G7 eyes Lebanon
items.summary: President Trump said the Iran deal is 'not final' and warned of renewed strikes, while G7 leaders called for a wider ceasefire. JD Vance said the text would be released by Friday.
items.whyItMatters: Any sign the fragile Iran truce could break puts a floor under oil and the safe-haven dollar.

items.headline: Strait of Hormuz reopens, but shippers stay cautious on safety
items.summary: The vital oil shipping lane has reopened, though operators and insurers are waiting to see whether passage is genuinely safe before returning.
items.whyItMatters: Hormuz carries a fifth of the world's oil — its reopening is why crude and inflation fears are easing today.

items.headline: G7 agrees new steps to pressure Russia as Trump weighs more sanctions
items.summary: Leaders closed the G7 summit agreeing fresh measures against Moscow, with Trump saying the US is 'looking at' further sanctions.
items.whyItMatters: Tighter Russia sanctions can jolt energy markets and the currencies of countries that trade with Moscow.

items.headline: Starmer signals a cabinet job for Andy Burnham amid leadership chatter
items.summary: The PM called the Greater Manchester mayor a 'huge asset' as Labour MPs openly float Burnham as a future leader, should he win an upcoming byelection.
items.whyItMatters: Leadership uncertainty at the top of government is a slow drag on the pound until the picture settles.

items.headline: Fed holds rates steady in Kevin Warsh's debut as chair
items.summary: The Federal Reserve kept rates unchanged at new Chair Kevin Warsh's first meeting, with no surprise in the decision itself — attention is on how he reshapes the Fed's communication.
items.whyItMatters: How Warsh signals the path ahead, more than today's hold, will set the dollar's direction into year-end.

items.headline: UK inflation unexpectedly holds steady ahead of the BoE decision
items.summary: British inflation came in flat rather than cooling further, complicating the Bank of England's rate call due shortly.
items.whyItMatters: Stickier inflation makes BoE cuts harder to justify, a near-term support for the pound.

items.headline: ECB's Sleijpen says a repeat of 2022 inflation is unlikely but not ruled out
items.summary: The ECB policymaker struck a calmer tone on price pressures, suggesting the worst of the war-driven inflation scare may be passing.
items.whyItMatters: A less alarmed ECB means less pressure to keep hiking — a mild headwind for the euro.

items.headline: Russian household inflation expectations ease to 12.4% in June
items.summary: Russia's central bank reported a drop in households' inflation expectations, a small sign of cooling price pressure.
items.whyItMatters: Lower expectations give the Russian central bank room to ease, which tends to weaken the rouble.

items.headline: Chile's central bank trims growth forecast and lifts inflation view
items.summary: Chile cut its 2026 GDP growth estimate while nudging up its inflation projection, a stagflationary tilt.
items.whyItMatters: Weaker growth with firmer inflation is an awkward backdrop that tends to pressure the peso.

items.headline: Revolut wins UAE central bank licences for full digital banking
items.summary: Revolut secured licences from the UAE central bank to offer full digital banking services in the Emirates.
items.whyItMatters: More licensed digital banks in the Gulf means cheaper, faster ways to move money in and out of the region.

items.headline: Fed expected to hold as markets parse Warsh's first meeting
items.summary: FXStreet notes the Fed was widely expected to keep rates steady, with the real signal coming from how the new chair frames policy going forward.
items.whyItMatters: The dollar's next leg hinges on Warsh's tone, not the rate itself — expect choppy trading around the press conference.

items.headline: The BoJ hiked to 1.0% — so why is the yen still falling?
items.summary: Analysts say the Bank of Japan's latest hike to 1.0% wasn't enough to trigger a sustained yen recovery, with wide rate gaps to the dollar still dominating.
items.whyItMatters: Even with Japan finally raising rates, the yen stays weak — good for travel to Japan, painful for yen holders.

items.headline: Euro slips below 1.1600 with the Fed in the spotlight
items.summary: EUR/USD gave back recent gains, easing under 1.1600 as traders turned cautious ahead of the Fed decision and Warsh's debut.
items.whyItMatters: 1.1600 is the line in the sand for the euro this week — a Fed surprise decides which way it breaks.

items.headline: US retail sales jump 0.9% in May, beating expectations
items.summary: American consumers spent more than expected in May, with sales up 0.9% versus the 0.5% forecast and broad strength across categories.
items.whyItMatters: A strong consumer gives the Fed less reason to cut, which tends to support the dollar.

items.headline: New Zealand dollar weakens as confidence hits a two-year low
items.summary: NZD/USD slipped toward 0.5820 as investors cut risk ahead of the Fed and local confidence fell to its lowest since 2023.
items.whyItMatters: The kiwi is a classic risk gauge — its slide shows investors playing defence into the Fed.

items.headline: Canadian dollar near lows as oil slump and Fed outlook weigh
items.summary: USD/CAD held around 1.4010 with the loonie pressured by falling oil and a cautious Fed backdrop.
items.whyItMatters: Cheaper oil pulls the commodity-linked loonie down — the flip side of today's easing inflation story.

items.headline: ABN AMRO: a new Fed chapter could reshape the dollar's path
items.summary: ABN AMRO economists argue the leadership change at the Fed could influence the dollar's direction over the coming months.
items.whyItMatters: A new Fed chair can shift the whole rate outlook — the single biggest driver of the dollar you hold or send.

items.headline: Oil prices fall sharply as Iran supply outlook improves
items.summary: Crude dropped, holding below $80, as the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz and an improving Iran supply picture eased fears.
items.whyItMatters: Falling oil cools the inflation that drove this year's rate hikes — the day's biggest cross-currency theme.

items.headline: Egypt's pound becomes the world's top currency as the oil shock reverses
items.summary: The Egyptian pound has rallied to lead global currencies as falling energy prices unwind the pressure that had weighed on it.
items.whyItMatters: A surging pound changes the math on transfers to Egypt — fewer pounds per dollar sent home.

items.headline: Sterling looks past cooler inflation with the BoE in focus
items.summary: The pound shrugged off softer inflation details as traders set up for the Bank of England's decision.
items.whyItMatters: Sterling is in wait-and-see mode — the BoE, not the data, is what moves it next.

items.headline: Indian rupee gains 31 paise as crude prices slide
items.summary: The rupee strengthened as falling oil eased India's import-cost burden ahead of the Fed decision.
items.whyItMatters: India imports most of its oil, so cheaper crude is a direct boost to the rupee — and to what your rupees buy.

items.headline: South Africa's May inflation print makes the SARB's next call harder
items.summary: A tricky inflation reading has complicated the South African Reserve Bank's upcoming rate decision.
items.whyItMatters: Rate uncertainty tends to add swings to the rand — worth noting if you send money to South Africa.

items.headline: Hormuz reopening seen boosting the ringgit and easing inflation
items.summary: Analysts expect the reopened shipping lane and softer oil to support Malaysia's ringgit and cool price pressures.
items.whyItMatters: Lower energy costs tend to lift oil-importing Asian currencies like the ringgit.

items.headline: World Bank lends Argentina $900m as its inflation crisis grinds on
items.summary: The World Bank extended $900m to Argentina as the country battles persistent high inflation.
items.whyItMatters: External funding is a lifeline for the peso, but won't fix the underlying inflation that erodes it.

items.headline: The best early Amazon Prime Day deals so far
items.summary: The Verge is tracking the strongest discounts ahead of Amazon's Prime Day, which runs June 23–27 this year.
items.whyItMatters: Early deals often match Prime Day prices — buying now beats the day-of scramble.

items.headline: 15 of Wirecutter's favorite Father's Day gifts are on sale now
items.summary: Wirecutter rounded up tested Father's Day gift picks that are currently discounted, for the dad who insists he needs nothing.
items.whyItMatters: Father's Day is days away — tested-and-discounted is the sweet spot for a last-minute gift.

items.headline: The best Apple deals you can already grab ahead of Prime Day
items.summary: AirPods Pro have fallen to nearly the price of the standard noise-cancelling AirPods 4 in early Prime Day discounting.
items.whyItMatters: Apple gear rarely drops much — an early dip is a genuine reason to buy now rather than wait.

items.headline: Paramount+ is $2 for two months of ad-free streaming
items.summary: New and former subscribers can get ad-free Paramount+ at a steep discount through June 25th.
items.whyItMatters: A cheap two-month window is perfect for bingeing a series then cancelling — no long commitment.

items.headline: Toy Story 5 makes its villain the phones and tablets kids love
items.summary: The new sequel returns to the franchise's roots, pitting Woody and Buzz against the screens competing for children's attention.
items.whyItMatters: One for the family calendar — a summer outing that won't break the budget.

items.headline: Jackery unveils its 'world's slimmest' fridge backup battery
items.summary: Jackery's new slim battery is designed to fit beside or behind a fridge to keep it running during a power outage.
items.whyItMatters: A practical buy if storm-season outages are a worry where you live.
